pattern: The first player wins exactly when the starting XOR is already zero or the count of numbers is even, a tiny parity rule that collapses the whole game tree.
hints: Look at two trivial situations first: what happens when the total XOR is already zero before any move, and what the parity of the count implies.
If the overall XOR is zero the first player wins immediately; otherwise the danger is being forced to make the XOR zero by your own move.
The first player wins if and only if the initial XOR is zero or the number of elements is even.

edge_inputs.description: first statement example, odd count with nonzero xor loses
second statement example, even count wins
third statement example, xor already zero
single nonzero element forces a losing move
single zero is already xor zero so first player wins
odd count of identical values xors to that value
even count with nonzero xor still wins on parity
even count whose xor is zero wins both ways

stress.description: random arrays of varying length and value to exercise both parity and xor-zero branches
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 16) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: kept short and low-valued so the brute oracle game search stays fast
